label: Google Search Console
description: Pull SEO performance data — clicks, impressions, queries, and index coverage — from Google Search Console into Anton.

form.title: Connect Google Search Console
form.subtitle: Authorize Anton to read your site's search performance and index coverage data.

methods.label: Sign in with Google (OAuth 2.0)
methods.description: Connect via OAuth 2.0 using your own Google Cloud OAuth client. Anton opens a browser consent screen — no passwords stored, revocable at any time from your Google Account.
methods.how_to:
> **Pick the OAuth client type that matches your Anton shell:**
>
> - **Desktop app (Anton.app on macOS / Windows)** → in *Application type*, pick **Desktop app**. Google auto-allows loopback redirects, so no redirect-URI registration is needed.
> - **Web (browser-hosted Anton)** → pick **Web application** instead, then under *Authorized redirect URIs* add `http://127.0.0.1:26866/v1/connectors/oauth/callback` (substitute your server origin if you're behind a reverse proxy or running on a non-default port).
>
> The walkthrough below was written for the desktop path. If you're on web, read every "Desktop app" reference as "Web application" and make sure the redirect URI above is registered before clicking **Submit**.

## Sign in with Google (OAuth 2.0 — Bring Your Own Client)

Anton doesn't ship a hosted OAuth client for Google Search Console yet, so you'll create one in Google Cloud Console in about 3 minutes.

### 1. Enable the API

1. Open [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/) and pick or create a project.
2. Go to **APIs & Services → Library**, search for **Google Search Console API**, and click **Enable**.

### 2. Configure the OAuth consent screen

1. Go to **APIs & Services → OAuth consent screen**.
2. Choose **External** (or **Internal** if this is a Workspace org) and click **Create**.
3. Fill in app name (e.g. `Anton`) and your email, then save.
4. Under **Scopes**, add `webmasters.readonly` and `userinfo.email`.
5. Under **Test users**, add the Google account you'll authorize with.

### 3. Create an OAuth client ID

1. Go to **APIs & Services → Credentials → Create credentials → OAuth client ID**.
2. Choose **Desktop app** as the application type.
3. Copy the **Client ID** and **Client secret** — paste them into the fields below.

### 4. Authorize

Click **Submit**. Anton opens your browser to Google's consent screen. Sign in with the account that has Search Console access, review the permissions (`Read your Search Console data` and `See your email address`), and click **Continue**.

### Notes

- The account must be a verified owner or full user of the property in Search Console.
- To revoke access later, visit [Third-party apps with account access](https://myaccount.google.com/permissions) and remove the entry.
- If your consent screen is in **Testing** status, tokens expire after 7 days — publish the app to remove that limit.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth Client ID
fields.description: From Google Cloud Console → APIs & Services → Credentials. Use a Desktop app OAuth client.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
fields.description: Paired with the client ID — copied from the same credential row in Cloud Console.

methods.label: Service Account
methods.description: Use a Google service account JSON key for server-to-server access. Ideal for automated pipelines where interactive login isn't possible.
methods.how_to:
## Service Account Access

Use this method for automated or headless setups. The service account must be granted access to the Search Console property by a verified owner.

### 1. Create a service account

1. Open [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/) → **IAM & Admin → Service Accounts → Create service account**.
2. Give it a name like `anton-search-console` and click **Done**.
3. Open the account, go to the **Keys** tab, and click **Add key → Create new key → JSON**. Download the file.

### 2. Enable the API

1. Go to **APIs & Services → Library**, search for **Google Search Console API**, and click **Enable**.

### 3. Grant the service account access in Search Console

1. Open [Google Search Console](https://search.google.com/search-console).
2. Select the property you want Anton to access.
3. Go to **Settings → Users and permissions → Add user**.
4. Enter the service account's email address (ends in `.iam.gserviceaccount.com`) and set permission to **Full**.

### 4. Paste the key

Copy the entire contents of the downloaded JSON key file and paste it into **Service Account JSON** below.

### Notes

- The service account email must be added to each Search Console property individually.
- Rotate keys in Cloud Console if the key is ever exposed. Re-paste the new JSON in Anton to reconnect.

fields.name: service_account_json
fields.label: Service Account JSON
fields.description: Paste the entire contents of the JSON key file downloaded from Google Cloud Console.

fields.name: site_url
fields.label: Property URL
fields.description: The Search Console property URL the service account has been granted access to (e.g. https://www.example.com/ or sc-domain:example.com).
